op.name: logaddexp
op.summary: Logarithm of the sum of exponentiations of the inputs.
op.signature: we.logaddexp(x1, x2, /, out=None, *, where=True, casting='same_kind', order='K', dtype=None, subok=True[, signature])

blocks.text: Logarithm of the sum of exponentiations of the inputs.

Calculates ``log(exp(x1) + exp(x2))``. This function is useful in
statistics where the calculated probabilities of events may be so small
as to exceed the range of normal floating point numbers.  In such cases
the logarithm of the calculated probability is stored. This function
allows adding probabilities stored in such a fashion.

desc_blocks.text:
Input values.
If ``x1.shape != x2.shape``, they must be broadcastable to a common
shape (which becomes the shape of the output).

items.name: out
items.type: ndarray, None, or tuple of ndarray and None, optional

desc_blocks.text:
A location into which the result is stored. If provided, it must have
a shape that the inputs broadcast to. If not provided or None,
a freshly-allocated array is returned. A tuple (possible only as a
keyword argument) must have length equal to the number of outputs.

This condition is broadcast over the input. At locations where the
condition is True, the `out` array will be set to the ufunc result.
Elsewhere, the `out` array will retain its original value.
Note that if an uninitialized `out` array is created via the default
``out=None``, locations within it where the condition is False will
remain uninitialized.

For other keyword-only arguments, see the
:ref:`ufunc docs <ufuncs.kwargs>`.

Logarithm of ``exp(x1) + exp(x2)``.
This is a scalar if both `x1` and `x2` are scalars.

blocks.code:
>>> import numpy as np
>>> prob1 = np.log(1e-50)
>>> prob2 = np.log(2.5e-50)
>>> prob12 = np.logaddexp(prob1, prob2)
>>> prob12
-113.87649168120691
>>> np.exp(prob12)
3.5000000000000057e-50

blocks.text: logaddexp2 — Logarithm of the sum of exponentiations of inputs in base 2.
